message: I've never been the 'prepared person' in my friend group—I'm usually the one showing up five minutes late asking if anyone has a phone charger. But last spring when that tornado warning hit, I actually *had* a weather radio, a go-bag with water and a flashlight, and I knew exactly where to move the kids. And you know what? It felt amazing. Not in a smug way—more like relief mixed with 'wait, I actually did this?' My partner looked at me like I'd grown a second head. It turns out being ready for a storm doesn't require you to become a totally different person. Just a few thoughtful moves made when the sun is shining and there's zero pressure. I found this little refresher really helpful for keeping it all in one place. Have you ever had a moment where being prepared made you feel genuinely calmer?
